nelsonTwist.title: Port / Nelson Twist
nelsonTwist.tagline: Invert and recirculate the loaded clip, then warp it through a repeated routed branch for a hard-edged feedback twist.
nelsonTwist.videoIntent: Best on graphic motion, silhouettes, or footage with clear contour so the warped repeat branch stays assertive instead of muddy.
nelsonTwist.audioIntent: The coupled sound should feel like phase-twisted feedback pressure, not a random chorus pasted over the clip.

pixelscape.title: Port / Pixelscape
pixelscape.tagline: Push the clip through a routed scale-and-hue field, then lay a keyed tinted branch back over it like a floating matte.
pixelscape.videoIntent: Best on faces, gestures, and layered light where a routed zoom field can pull the image apart before the overprint lands.
pixelscape.audioIntent: The coupled sound should shimmer and widen through a real routed motion branch rather than just blurring into polite detune.

disintegration.title: Port / Disintegration
disintegration.tagline: Shear the clip through routed displacement and vertical motion control, then pull it inward under a pale collapsing tint.
disintegration.videoIntent: Best on bodies, edges, and directional movement where the routed motion branches can visibly tear the image apart.
disintegration.audioIntent: The coupled sound should feel like a dissolving stereo field with real motion pressure, not generic reverb wash.

acidBusSeat.title: Port / Acid Bus Seat
acidBusSeat.tagline: Split the clip across buses, force one branch into routed held-window grime, then shove the main image through a cyan acid scale field.
acidBusSeat.videoIntent: Best on busy footage, instruments, or bodies where a pixel-torn side bus can keep fighting the main branch instead of tinting it politely.
acidBusSeat.audioIntent: The coupled sound should feel restless and acid-leaning, with the dirty routed bus actively pushing the main line rather than just doubling it.

glitchySlitScan.title: Port / Glitchy Slit Scan
glitchySlitScan.tagline: Carve a moving slit branch out of the clip, push it through routed held-window tearing, then lay that memory strip back onto the clean image.
glitchySlitScan.videoIntent: Best on faces, webcams, or close framing where a moving slit branch can read as motion memory instead of static masking.
glitchySlitScan.audioIntent: The coupled sound should feel like a narrow moving spectral cut with persistence and scrape, not a nearly dry image-to-audio mapping.

haloKey.title: Finish / Halo Key
haloKey.tagline: Lift the clip through a keyed halo branch, then lay the glow back over the clean source.
haloKey.videoIntent: Best on portraits, bodies, lights, or high-contrast motion where a bright matte can separate without turning to soup.
haloKey.audioIntent: The coupled result should widen and sweeten the keyed peaks rather than sounding like a generic chorus pasted on top.

rgbSpill.title: Finish / RGB Spill
rgbSpill.tagline: Split the source into red and blue matte branches, collapse one into a weighted key, then spill the tinted composite back into the clip.
rgbSpill.videoIntent: Use on clips with edges, silhouettes, or costume contrast so the split-matte color spill stays graphic instead of muddy.
rgbSpill.audioIntent: The coupled sound should feel spectrally separated and tense, with the low and high-band branches rejoining as one gesture.

shadowMatte.title: Finish / Shadow Matte
shadowMatte.tagline: Carve the source through an inverted alpha-or-luma matte, then feed the dark cut back as a stylized finish layer.
shadowMatte.videoIntent: Best on moody footage, backlight, or dark wardrobe where the shadow carve should feel deliberate instead of merely underexposed.
shadowMatte.audioIntent: The coupled sound should narrow into a darker contour and then reopen with controlled texture, not collapse into broadband mud.

velasco.title: Port / Velasco
velasco.tagline: Split the loaded clip across two buses, tint each branch differently, then drive a repeated diff field with live low-band audio scale.
velasco.videoIntent: Best on footage with crisp edges, bodies, or graphic motion so the repeated lattice and bus-difference contours stay legible.
velasco.audioIntent: The coupled result should feel agitated and rhythmic, with low-band energy physically breathing the scale rather than reading as random wobble.

marianne.title: Port / Marianne
marianne.tagline: Fold the loaded clip through stepped kaleido symmetry, subtle hue drift, and self-rotating modulation.
marianne.videoIntent: Best on clips with strong contour, gesture, or silhouette so the rotating kaleido and chroma treatment stay legible.
marianne.audioIntent: The coupled result should feel shimmering and spatially alive rather than washed out or vibrato-heavy.

tunnel.title: Tunnel Feedback
tunnel.tagline: Pull the uploaded clip into a receding audio-visual tunnel.
tunnel.videoIntent: Best on footage with perspective lines, camera drift, or strong center framing.
tunnel.audioIntent: Feedback and light modulation should add a receding, hollow persistence without washing out transients.

bloom.title: Bloom Ghost
bloom.tagline: Smear highlights and sustain the clip into a soft trailing bloom.
bloom.videoIntent: Works best on bright footage, silhouettes, practical lights, or stage shots.
bloom.audioIntent: The coupled delay should widen and soften the sound without collapsing the attack.

lattice.title: Lattice Geometry
lattice.tagline: Fold the clip into a repeated kaleido lattice with controlled feedback.
lattice.videoIntent: Use on rhythmic motion, architecture, fabric, or any source with strong repeating detail.
lattice.audioIntent: The coupled motion should feel grid-like and pulsed rather than smeared or random.

chaos.title: Chaos Spiral
chaos.tagline: Push the clip into unstable rotational feedback and chroma drift.
chaos.videoIntent: Best for energetic footage where loss of stability is part of the look, not a defect.
chaos.audioIntent: The coupled sound should become more agitated and unstable without collapsing into clipping.

ghost.title: Ghost Persistence
ghost.tagline: Leave spectral after-images that trail behind the moving subject.
ghost.videoIntent: Use on portraits, bodies, or slow camera motion where temporal echoes stay legible.
ghost.audioIntent: The sound should feel suspended and haunted, with persistence more than obvious pitch shift.

kaleido.title: Kaleido Prism
kaleido.tagline: Turn the clip into a multi-sided prism with restrained motion feedback.
kaleido.videoIntent: Works well on hands, faces, instruments, and centered action that can tolerate symmetry.
kaleido.audioIntent: The coupled motion should read as prismatic widening rather than pure delay wash.

temporalBloomGhost.title: Temporal Bloom Ghost
temporalBloomGhost.tagline: Hold the clip inside a soft multi-frame memory field, then let the brightest gestures bloom back through it.
temporalBloomGhost.videoIntent: Best on portraits, lights, and slow-to-medium movement where time drag can stay legible instead of collapsing into noise.
temporalBloomGhost.audioIntent: The grain cloud should widen and hang in the air while feedback stays present but controlled.

slitScanEcho.title: Slit-Scan Echo
slitScanEcho.tagline: Slice the frame into vertical time bands, then let each band replay a slightly different moment of the clip.
slitScanEcho.videoIntent: Best on faces, dancers, and handheld movement where vertical temporal drift reads as a deliberate scan rather than random tearing.
slitScanEcho.audioIntent: The grains should feel more sparse and suspended, with enough space for the time slicing to read as one gesture.

lumaTimeSmear.title: Luma Time Smear
lumaTimeSmear.tagline: Let bright and dark structures pull from different moments of the clip, then tighten the result into a shaped contour.
lumaTimeSmear.videoIntent: Best on graphic lighting, silhouettes, and footage with strong tonal separation so luma-indexed time drag stays articulate.
lumaTimeSmear.audioIntent: The grain cloud should stay present and musical while the shared feedback remains a supporting texture instead of the headline.

edgeFeedback.title: Edge Feedback
edgeFeedback.tagline: Let the clip's own contours decide where memory and displacement are allowed to bite, so edges drag while flat regions stay calm.
edgeFeedback.videoIntent: Best on silhouettes, architecture, and body motion where contour-led tearing reads as authored structure instead of general blur.
edgeFeedback.audioIntent: The grains should stay compact and articulate while the shared delay mirrors the visual drag without taking over.

contourBloom.title: Contour Bloom
contourBloom.tagline: Pull luminous contour and luma structure out of the clip, then let those lines bloom and drift without washing the whole frame.
contourBloom.videoIntent: Best on highlights, costume contrast, and practical lights where contour-led glow can stay graphic instead of turning to fog.
contourBloom.audioIntent: The grain field should stay airy and musical, with the shared delay acting as a halo rather than a slap.

datamoshSmear.title: Datamosh Smear
datamoshSmear.tagline: Track motion between frames, then let that field drag the clip into blocky directional tears instead of generic blur.
datamoshSmear.videoIntent: Best on gestures, handheld movement, dancers, and directional camera motion where flow-driven tearing can read as motion memory instead of simple distortion.
datamoshSmear.audioIntent: The grains should stay clipped and present while the shared delay mirrors the smear as pressure, not as a washy headline effect.

datamoshHold.title: Datamosh Hold
datamoshHold.tagline: Hold the last keyframe in a self-feedback buffer and let the clip's motion field smear that held image along the next predicted P-frames.
datamoshHold.videoIntent: Best on cuts, fast camera moves, and percussive motion where the held keyframe can drift visibly before the motion threshold releases it back to live.
datamoshHold.audioIntent: The grain cloud should sit just below the held image so that the visual freeze reads as a delay, not as a dropout, and the shared delay tail should match the smear length.

flowMelt.title: Flow Melt
flowMelt.tagline: Use the clip's motion field as a soft liquid pull, then let temporal memory and contour structure turn that pull into a polished melt.
flowMelt.videoIntent: Best on faces, fabric, smoke, and broad moving surfaces where a directional melt can stay legible without reading as a hard glitch.
flowMelt.audioIntent: The grain cloud should stay airy and suspended while the shared delay adds a quiet tail behind the visual drag.

motionBloomGhost.title: Motion Bloom Ghost
motionBloomGhost.tagline: Let motion pull a soft liquid ghost through the clip, then hold that ghost in a luminous temporal halo.
motionBloomGhost.videoIntent: Best on medium movement, practical lights, and faces where motion memory can stay supple instead of turning into hard glitch blocks.
motionBloomGhost.audioIntent: The grain cloud should hover and widen with the motion bloom rather than turn into a thick wash.

kaleidoFeedbackTunnel.title: Kaleido Feedback Tunnel
kaleidoFeedbackTunnel.tagline: Fold the clip into a restrained prism, then let temporal replay and feedback turn that prism into a tunneled corridor.
kaleidoFeedbackTunnel.videoIntent: Best on centered movement, limbs, and architecture where symmetry can feel intentional and the tunnel still has a clear subject.
kaleidoFeedbackTunnel.audioIntent: The grains should pulse around the tunnel rather than fill it completely, with the shared delay reading as depth.

freezeFeedback.title: Freeze Feedback
freezeFeedback.tagline: Hold fragments of the clip almost in place, then let contour-led feedback decide what continues to move inside the freeze.
freezeFeedback.videoIntent: Best on stillness, pauses, and sparse action where a near-held frame can keep a strong silhouette while only selected regions keep breathing.
freezeFeedback.audioIntent: The granulator should thin out and lengthen so the scene feels suspended rather than busy.

granularVideoCloud.title: Granular Video Cloud
granularVideoCloud.tagline: Shape the clip into a soft contour cloud while the granulator becomes the audible centre of gravity instead of an afterthought.
granularVideoCloud.videoIntent: Best on broad moving forms, haze, cloth, and low-contrast footage where contour and motion can build a visible cloud instead of a hard edge effect.
granularVideoCloud.audioIntent: The grain engine should read immediately as the instrument, with the video moving in the same density and spread family.

grainField.title: Grain Field
grainField.tagline: A dense field of small audible grains paired with a softly-banded video texture — the cleanest demonstration of the granulator-first product shape.
grainField.videoIntent: Best on slow-moving footage with broad tonal areas where the gentle posterize banding and feedback smear can read as visible texture instead of noise.
grainField.audioIntent: The granulator runs at ~35 grains/sec with modest jitter and stereo spread, so individual grains are audible as a steady shimmering stream rather than a smoothed cloud.

vortexField.title: Vortex Field
vortexField.tagline: Authored point-vortex velocity field swirls the clip while feedback trails hold the motion in place.
vortexField.videoIntent: Best on footage with strong colour contrast and gentle motion — faces, foliage, water — where the Biot-Savart swirl reads as designed flow rather than damage.
vortexField.audioIntent: Quiet granulator bed so the visual swirl carries the piece; feedback delay stays light.

curlTurbulence.title: Curl Turbulence
curlTurbulence.tagline: Divergence-free curl-of-noise warps the clip like slow fluid — no piling, no thinning, just flow.
curlTurbulence.videoIntent: Best on footage with mid-frequency texture (skin, foliage, fabric) where the turbulence reads as motion rather than blur.
curlTurbulence.audioIntent: Granulator stays in a wide cloud so the audio breathes with the visual drift; feedback delay light.

vortexPacketStorm.title: Vortex Packet Storm
vortexPacketStorm.tagline: Broad slow swirls anchor the frame while a dense cloud of fast eddies stipples the surface.
vortexPacketStorm.videoIntent: Best on busy, high-contrast footage — crowds, water, foliage in wind — where the two-band field separates into a readable macro/micro structure.
vortexPacketStorm.audioIntent: Granulator pushed denser and slightly pitched, mirroring the micro-eddy stipple in the audio domain.

saddleSweep.title: Saddle Sweep
saddleSweep.tagline: Oriented saddle currents sweep the clip in directional streaks rather than swirls.
saddleSweep.videoIntent: Best on footage with existing horizontal or diagonal motion — landscapes from a moving vehicle, dance — where the saddle axes amplify the existing direction.
saddleSweep.audioIntent: Granulator in classic mode with light spray so the sound stays focused; feedback delay adds spatial depth.

fieldOrbitLab.title: Field Orbit Lab
fieldOrbitLab.tagline: Internal reference patch for the new field-operator pack: fold, spiral, sink/source, and ripple in one feedback-safe chain.
fieldOrbitLab.videoIntent: Authored field study for auditioning how the cheaper coordinate-warp operators stack under feedback.
fieldOrbitLab.audioIntent: Keep the granulator and delay present but secondary so the visual field motion stays readable while testing modulation.

zero.title: Near Clean
zero.tagline: Return the clip close to a neutral baseline for comparison and reset.
zero.videoIntent: Use before auditioning another program or when checking how far a clip has drifted.
zero.audioIntent: The sound should settle back toward the untreated source without muting the chain.
